trivia_bullets.text: Khans of Tarkir (KTK) released 2014-09-26 as an expansion set centered on the plane of Tarkir — a world ruled by five warring clans after its dragons went extinct. Morph was the block's signature shared mechanic, chosen by lead designer Mark Rosewater because its hidden-information gameplay mirrored the block's themes of mystery and uncertain allegiance [Wizards: 'Khans of Tarkir Design Handoff, Part 1'].

Flavor text reads: "It strode through the clash of dragons, the fall of Ugin, and the rise of the khans." — no speaker attributed. The line locates the Witness as a pre-lapsarian artifact that predates the dragons' extinction: the clash of dragons refers to the ancient war between dragonlords and khans; the fall of Ugin refers to the Spirit Dragon's defeat by Nicol Bolas roughly 1,280 years before the set's present; the rise of the khans marks the power vacuum that followed. The golem is older than Tarkir's memory of itself [Scryfall].

Costs 6 generic mana for a 4/4 with Morph 5, making it one of the more expensive morph creatures in KTK. It sits at EDHREC rank 28,724 — effectively unplayed in Commander — which reflects that a vanilla 4/4 for 6 competes poorly with cheaper options, and the morph cost of 5 is only marginally easier to cast than the face-up cost [Scryfall EDHREC rank field].

Illustrated by Izzy (full legal name Izzy Medrano), a prolific MTG artist known for large, imposing creature art. Witness of the Ages is the sole printing of this card across any MTG set — no reprints exist as of 2026 [Scryfall prints_search_uri: single result].
